understanding.goal_understanding: The goal of the game/level was to place every selectable piece onto the goal tiles marked with '+', and win only when all goal tiles are occupied by pieces at the same time before the step budget runs out.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: There is an active/selected piece that receives movement commands. MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN attempt to move the selected piece in that direction, but the move can be blocked by walls/obstacles/bounds (resulting in no position change). CLICK [x,y] attempts to move the selected piece to the clicked grid coordinate; if the destination is reachable/valid (not blocked), the piece relocates to that exact tile, but if the click target is blocked or otherwise invalid, the piece does not move. Each action consumes 1 step regardless of whether it changes position.

orient_history: CLICK selects a different movable piece; movement applies only to the selected piece. Since current piece is immobile, switching selection may allow progress toward the goal.

CLICK likely allows repositioning/teleporting the selected selectable to the clicked location (or otherwise interact beyond the wall-locked pocket). Since movement is fully blocked, solving likely requires clicking the goal to place a selectable onto it.

Hypothesis: CLICK can place/duplicate the currently selected piece onto a clicked empty/goal tile, but only when the target shares the same row or column (pixel-aligned x or y) with the selected piece. Turn1 succeeded along same y=21; Turn2 failed because it was not aligned.

CLICK appears to attempt to place the selected 3x3 piece with its top-left anchored at the clicked pixel position (must be grid-aligned and not overlap any wall). Prior failed clicks likely targeted positions that would make the 3x3 overlap surrounding walls; the correct top-left for the 3x3 goal cavity should be at cell [11,5] = pixels [33,15].

CLICK likely selects a selectable piece when you click on its position; MOVE_* attempts to move the currently selected piece by one cell (3 px) but is prevented by walls/collisions. Since the selected piece is immobile, we should switch selection to the other selectable and test its available moves.

New hypothesis (discarding prior): CLICK is not the primary way to progress here; instead, MOVE actions may change which piece is controlled (cycle selection) even when physical movement is blocked, allowing us to act from the other selectable that may have a path toward the goal.

Movement arrows move by one cell (3 pixels) when not blocked. CLICK attempts to move the currently selected piece to the clicked destination (likely via path/slide/teleport rules), but it fails if the destination is unreachable/blocked. To make progress, we should CLICK directly on a goal tile coordinate rather than nearby non-goal cells.

CLICK likely changes which selectable is controlled (appearing as the 'selected piece moved' to the clicked selectable’s position). Arrow moves shift the currently selected piece by 3 pixels (1 cell) when not blocked.

CLICK appears to swap the selected piece with the other selectable (earlier clicks moved the selected piece onto the other selectable’s position). Arrow moves translate the currently-selected piece by 3 pixels (1 cell) when not blocked.

CLICK on a selectable switches selection (so it looks like the selected piece 'moves' to that selectable). Movement options may differ per selectable depending on its local surroundings, so I should switch to the other piece and re-check available moves toward the goal.

CLICK likely attempts to relocate the currently selected piece to the clicked coordinate if that destination is valid/reachable under the level’s movement/collision rules; if blocked, nothing happens. Since the current selected piece at [18,21] is fully boxed in, progress likely requires switching to the other selectable at [12,21] and trying movement/relocation from there.

Hypothesis reset: CLICK teleports the selected piece directly to the clicked location only if that location is a valid reachable/allowed empty area; otherwise nothing happens. When a piece is snug against walls on all sides, only directional MOVE might reveal whether there is any hidden pass-through/slide rule or whether we must first switch pieces (likely via CLICK) to escape.

CLICK appears to relocate the currently selected piece directly to the clicked pixel position if that destination is valid (not colliding), without requiring step-by-step adjacency movement; some CLICK targets fail due to walls/invalid space. Since cardinal moves are fully blocked here, we should probe for a valid open destination to the right of the interior wall (wall spans x=24..30).

Hypothesis: MOVE_* moves 1 cell (3px) but can be blocked by walls; CLICK may either (a) switch selection when clicking a selectable, or (b) attempt a straight-line slide/relocation and fail if not allowed. Since the selected piece is fully blocked, next best is to try switching to the other piece via clicking it directly.

CLICK movement likely requires grid-aligned (multiples of 3) target positions; clicking non-aligned coordinates has no effect. To reach the goal, we should CLICK a pixel-aligned coordinate inside the goal region (e.g., [33,15]). After placing one selectable on the goal, we may need to switch/control the other selectable and place it onto the goal as well.

MOVE_* only works when there is empty adjacent space; right now the selected piece cannot move. CLICK likely can either (a) change which selectable is active when clicking another selectable, or (b) attempt a direct reposition/slide toward the clicked location if reachable; since we are trapped, the best progress is to switch control to the other selectable and try to navigate it toward the goal.

CLICKing on a selectable likely switches the currently-controlled piece (the engine then reports the 'selected piece moved' because the player reference changes). Since the currently selected piece is immobile (all moves blocked), switching to the other selectable may give access to different movement options or interactions.

New hypothesis (discarding prior): CLICK does not freely place a piece anywhere; it only moves to valid empty cells that are connected/allowed by the level’s collision/region rules. To make progress, we likely need to use MOVE actions to reposition within a region or to test whether blocked moves can still trigger a state change (e.g., sliding along walls, or switching which piece is active) before attempting different navigation toward the goal.

Hypothesis: MOVE_* only works when adjacent cell is not walled, but currently we are in a pocket. CLICK relocates the selected piece directly to the clicked coordinate if that destination is a valid empty/allowed tile (not a wall); clicking the other selectable’s location is a reliable way to reposition to a different pocket that may have exits.

MOVE actions only work for sliding by 3 pixels (1 cell) and are currently impossible due to adjacent walls. CLICK appears to reposition the selected piece directly to the clicked pixel coordinate if the destination placement is valid (not colliding with walls/blocked region).

CLICK selects (switches control to) the selectable at the clicked position; it does not teleport. Arrow moves then move only the currently selected piece, constrained by walls. To make progress we must select the other piece and try moving it toward the goal region.

Hypothesis: Arrow moves attempt to slide the currently selected 3x3 piece by 3 pixels (1 cell) but are blocked by walls; CLICK on a selectable switches selection to that piece. Need to find which selectable (if any) has a path to the goal region near [35,17].

CLICK seems to allow relocating the selected piece to specific coordinates (including onto/into the other selectable’s location), potentially swapping positions or moving through a non-cardinal rule; arrow moves are standard 3-pixel (1-cell) cardinal steps when not blocked.

New hypothesis (discarding prior): CLICK attempts to place/teleport the selected piece to an arbitrary empty location but fails if that destination overlaps walls or is outside the playable cavity. MOVE attempts to nudge the selected piece by one cell (3 px) but is blocked when any part would collide with wall bounds; current position is in a fully enclosed 3x3 pocket, so MOVE may confirm immobility and we likely need to relocate to a less-constrained spot afterward.

CLICK appears to change which selectable is currently controlled (select the piece at the clicked coordinates). MOVE_* moves only the currently selected piece by one cell (3 px) if not blocked. Since the current piece cannot move, we should switch control to the other piece and test its mobility toward the goal.

Hypothesis: MOVE_* shifts the currently selected 3x3 piece by one cell (3 px) but is blocked by collidable walls; CLICK on a selectable swaps/changes the active piece (and may swap positions if occupied), while CLICK on empty space only works if the destination is reachable/not blocked. To make progress, we need to control the other selectable and try actions from its location/cavity.

CLICK teleports the selected 3x3 piece to the clicked pixel position if that position is a valid, unblocked, grid-aligned top-left placement. The visible goal region corresponds to top-left pixel [33,15] (cell [11,5]); placing a 3x3 selectable there should cover all goal tiles at once and trigger WIN.

Hypothesis: MOVE_* attempts to translate the currently selected selectable by 1 cell (3 px) unless colliding with walls/other pieces. CLICK only swaps with (or selects) the other selectable when the click hits it; clicking empty/goal has no effect. If the currently selected piece is immobilized, we must switch to the other piece and try to navigate it toward the goal corridor.

CLICK likely attempts to reposition the selected piece to the clicked location if it is a valid (non-wall) placement aligned to the 3-pixel grid; clicks to non-aligned or colliding tiles result in no movement. MOVE_* is standard grid-step movement but we are enclosed, so CLICK placement onto the goal is the viable win path.

New hypothesis based only on observed behavior: CLICK is mainly a teleport/position-set mechanic that works only for certain valid tiles (often aligned to the 3px grid) and/or only within a reachable region; when the currently-selected piece is fully boxed in, MOVE inputs might be the way to change which selectable is active (selection/cycling), even if the current piece cannot move.

CLICK likely attempts to reposition the currently selected piece to the clicked location (if placement is valid); previous CLICKs sometimes moved the piece/selection, but clicks near the goal have been blocked so far—still, exact goal coordinates have not been tried.

CLICK likely changes which selectable is active when clicking on a selectable, while MOVE_* attempts to shift the active piece but is prevented by walls. With the step budget exhausted, further actions may be rejected, but I will test whether a CLICK can still interact (e.g., select/trigger) despite 0 steps.

history.summary: Level 1 step budget exhausted.
Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: The goal of the game/level was to place every selectable piece onto the goal tiles marked with '+', and win only when all goal tiles are occupied by pieces at the same time before the step budget runs out.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: There is an active/selected piece that receives movement commands. MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN attempt to move the selected piece in that direction, but the move can be blocked by walls/obstacles/bounds (resulting in no position change). CLICK [x,y] attempts to move the selected piece to the clicked grid coordinate; if the destination is reachable/valid (not blocked), the piece relocates to that exact tile, but if the click target is blocked or otherwise invalid, the piece does not move. Each action consumes 1 step regardless of whether it changes position.
